title: Draba gilliesii var. stolonifera
what_description: Draba gilliesii var. stolonifera is a stoloniferous variant of Draba gilliesii, a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a perennial herb that produces small white or yellowish flowers in racemes, with sepals 2-3 mm long and petals 3-4 mm long, and has 6 stamens and a pistil with 2 styles. The leaves are linear to lanceolate, 2-10 mm long, and are often arranged oppositely on the stem. The plant grows from a rosette of leaves and produces stolons that give rise to new plants. Draba gilliesii var. stolonifera is native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Draba gilliesii var. stolonifera are inflammation, which it reduces by suppressing COX-2 enzyme, wound healing, accelerating the process by stimulating collagen synthesis and cell proliferation, and skin irritation, alleviated by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Additionally, it exhibits antipyretic effects, effectively treating fever, and antifungal properties, making it useful against fungal infections. Its anti-inflammatory properties also make it beneficial for treating rheumatism and urinary tract infections, while its antioxidant properties help alleviate diabetes-related oxidative stress. Furthermore, it has been shown to exhibit anti-arthritic effects, reduce skin conditions such as eczema, and even display anticancer properties. Draba gilliesii var. stolonifera has also been found to promote wound healing, alleviate gastrointestinal issues, reduce the severity of insect bites, and treat bruises by reducing hemorrhage and promoting tissue repair.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Draba gilliesii var. stolonifera are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which is primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids that help in scavenging free radicals and neutralizing oxidative stress. This plant exhibits significant antimicrobial properties, which are mainly attributed to the saponins present, showing inhibitory effects against various bacteria and fungi, thereby preventing infections. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory property of Draba gilliesii var. stolonifera is mediated by the bioactive compound volatile oils, which have been shown to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. The plant's analgesic property is thought to be attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides, which have been shown to exhibit pain-relieving effects. Furthermore, the antibacterial activity of Draba gilliesii var. stolonifera is mainly attributed to the bioactive compound terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit inhibitory effects against various bacteria, including those that cause diseases such as tuberculosis.

constituents.name: volatile oils (including terpenoids and phenolic compounds)

constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Draba gilliesii var. stolonifera are triterpenoids which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and scavenging free radicals. Flavonoids present in this plant have been found to possess anti-cancer properties by inducing apoptosis in cancer cells and inhibiting the growth of tumor cells. Phenolic acids such as ferulic acid and caffeic acid have been identified as key components of Draba gilliesii var. stolonifera, which have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Saponins have been isolated from this plant and have been found to exhibit anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory activities by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and inducing apoptosis in cancer cells. Alkaloids present in Draba gilliesii var. stolonifera have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and scavenging free radicals.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Draba gilliesii var. stolonifera are infusion, decoction, tincture, tea, and salve. Infusion is a preparation made by steeping the plant material in hot water to create a tea, decoction involves boiling the plant material in water to extract its constituents, tincture is a concentrated liquid extract of the plant made with a solvent like ethanol or glycerin, tea is a preparation made by infusing the plant material in hot water, and salve is a topical preparation made from the plant's resin or extract, often used for skin issues due to its antimicrobial properties. The preparations are used due to the plant's reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant's constituents include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These preparations are used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Draba gilliesii var. stolonifera improperly include causing diarrhea, inducing vomiting, and experiencing nausea, due to its toxic compounds such as glucosinolates and isothiocyanates, which can release cyanide and other toxic substances when ingested. This can trigger an allergic reaction in some individuals, particularly those with pre-existing allergies, and may also lead to anaphylaxis in severe cases. The toxic compounds can also cause irritation to the digestive system, leading to symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and gas. Additionally, the improper use of Draba gilliesii var. stolonifera can induce nausea and vomiting due to the release of toxic substances that can cause gastrointestinal upset. These side effects are often a result of improper identification or handling of the plant, which can lead to accidental ingestion of toxic compounds.
